messages.content: Hi, could you help me to paraphrase and summarise this in a short paragraph.

Certainly! In brief, the request is to paraphrase and summarize a given piece of writing in a concise paragraph. However, without any specific text provided, I am unable to fulfill the request. Please provide the writing you would like paraphrased and summarized, and I will be happy to assist you.

To better scope the competencies required, reference was taken from SFEd and the following areas of practice were prioritised – E-pedagogy, Differentiated Instruction and CCE2021. Hence, staff who were competent or displayed flair in these areas were nominated as ICT champions, DI coaches and CCE specialised teachers respectively to drive the training and development programmes in the school.

To define the necessary skills needed for the job, the SFEd was consulted, and the focus was placed on three specific areas of practice: E-pedagogy, Differentiated Instruction, and CCE2021. Consequently, individuals who exhibited competency or aptitude in these areas were chosen as ICT champions, DI coaches, and CCE specialized teachers. Their role is to lead the training and development initiatives within the school.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this paragraph: In the planning process, Sec 1 Class Mentors (CM) who were able to deal with a diverse profile of students were first selected and paired up by the SL/YH, and the CM deployment was further discussed at SMC meeting.  IP HODs will then deploy the rest of their subject teachers accordingly, with common curriculum subject teachers skillful in DI deployed first.

During the planning stage, the Section 1 Class Mentors (CM) were carefully chosen based on their ability to handle students from various backgrounds. They were paired up by the Senior Level or Year Head (SL/YH), and the allocation of CMs was further deliberated in the School Management Committee (SMC) meeting. Next, the IP HODs (Integrated Programme Heads of Department) would allocate the other subject teachers according to the needs, ensuring that teachers skilled in Differentiated Instruction (DI) are assigned first among the common curriculum subjects.

Please help me to summarise and paraphrase this into a paragraph in my report: In September each year, all staff will complete the Staff Option Form, where they indicate their preference for deployment in the four areas - CM-ship, CCA, Committee and Exam duty. The YH, HOD CCA, SL and Assessment Committee IC will deploy the teachers accordingly, taking into consideration both their preferences and school needs. In particular, for both CCA & Committee, staff would be required to serve a minimum of two years before they can switch to a new committee. This is to allow teachers to be able to contribute effectively and ensure continuation of good work. Before the exercise, each committee IC would publicise the committee’s scope of work and the qualities of members they were looking for during the staff contact time. The Terms of Reference was also updated yearly to reflect the demands of the work.

Every September, all staff members are required to complete the Staff Option Form to indicate their desired deployment in four different areas: Class Mentorship (CM), Co-Curricular Activities (CCA), Committees, and Exam duties. The Year Head (YH), Head of Department for CCA, Senior Level (SL), and Assessment Committee In-Charge (IC) are responsible for assigning teachers to these areas, taking into consideration both the staff members' preferences and the school's needs. It is important to note that for both CCA and Committees, teachers must serve a minimum of two years before they can request a switch to a different committee. This policy ensures that teachers can make substantial contributions and maintain the continuity of good work. Prior to the deployment exercise, each committee IC promotes their committee's scope of work and the desired qualities of its members during staff contact time. Additionally, the Terms of Reference is updated annually to align with the evolving demands of the work.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this as a paragraph in my report: In deploying teachers for roles specific proficiency (RSP) such as Instructional Mentor, Teacher Counsellor, Sexuality Education Teachers, TSN, consultations were sought with various HOD and SL to shortlist teachers with the right dispositions and qualities, with consideration about their workload. Teachers were then approached to seek their opinions whether they would like to be developed in the new roles and their concerns raised and addressed before formalising their deployment.

In order to assign teachers to roles that require specific proficiency (RSP), such as Instructional Mentor, Teacher Counsellor, Sexuality Education Teachers, and TSN (Teacher Support Network), consultations were held with different Heads of Departments (HODs) and Senior Levels (SLs). This collaborative effort helped in selecting teachers who possessed the necessary dispositions and qualities required for these roles, while also considering their existing workload. Prior to finalizing their deployment, teachers were approached to gather their opinions and gauge their interest in developing themselves in these new roles. Any concerns raised by the teachers were also addressed to ensure a smooth transition into their designated positions.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this as a paragraph in my report: Staff were also identified and spoken to by SL if they could better support the primary schools or to have a stint at primary schools or even MOE HQ for exposure and further development. Experienced teachers were assigned mentor or buddy to these CLD teachers.

To enhance support for primary schools and provide opportunities for professional growth, the SL identified suitable staff members and engaged in conversations with them. These discussions aimed to explore options for the identified staff to either support primary schools, gain exposure at primary schools, or even pursue development opportunities at the Ministry of Education (MOE) Headquarters. Additionally, mentorship or buddy systems were established, with experienced teachers assigned to support and guide these staff members who were part of the Career and Leadership Development (CLD) program.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this as a paragraph in my report: Higher sub-grade or experienced officers were deployed as mentors for beginning and untrained teachers, with some of these officers also identified as DI coaches and ICT champions. They drove the school programmes with guidance from TL, SSD and HOD.

Experienced and senior officers were assigned as mentors for novice and untrained teachers, leveraging their expertise to provide guidance and support. Additionally, some of these officers were also designated as Differentiated Instruction (DI) coaches and Information and Communication Technology (ICT) champions. With the direction provided by Teacher Leaders (TL), School Staff Developers (SSD), and Heads of Department (HODs), they played a key role in leading and implementing various school programs.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this as a paragraph in my report:	At the end of each year, the staff resource plan is reviewed for updates, if any, and the HOD plan the deployment, with input and oversight of the SL. At the start of each year, the Principal also reiterates the school’s VMV to provide staff with a clear understanding of the desired outcomes.

The staff resource plan is annually reviewed for any necessary updates, with the Head of Departments (HODs) responsible for devising deployment strategies in consultation with Senior Levels (SLs) and under their supervision. In the beginning of each year, the Principal emphatically restates the school's Vision, Mission, and Values (VMV) to ensure that all staff members have a shared understanding of the desired outcomes.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this as a paragraph in my report: 	During the mid-year and end-of-year work review sessions, RO will check in with their reportees not only on their career development and performance, but also their mental well-being, especially in their ability in coping with their job scope.

At mid-year and end-of-year work review sessions, Reporting Officers (RO) conduct thorough assessments of their subordinates' career progression and job performance. Additionally, they also place emphasis on evaluating the mental well-being of the employees, particularly how effectively they manage the demands and responsibilities of their job scope. This comprehensive review ensures that both professional development and mental well-being are given equal consideration and attention.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this as a paragraph in my report: 	The Work Allocation Policy was reviewed yearly. For example, in 2022, clearer guidelines were made in the deployment of teachers for national examination duties - with a prioritisation for nomination of KPs and officers of subgrade GEO4 and above in key roles as CPE, PE or EPE. From 2023, the deployment of exam key roles will be discussed earlier by June, and disseminated to staff in Jan the following year for forward planning purposes.

On an annual basis, the Work Allocation Policy undergoes a review process. In 2022, the policy amendments aimed to clarify guidelines specifically pertaining to the assignment of teachers for national examination duties. Notably, there was a prioritization for the nomination of Key Personnel (KPs) and officers with a subgrade level of GEO4 or higher for crucial roles such as Chief Presiding Examiner (CPE), Presiding Examiner (PE), or Examiner Panel Evaluator (EPE). Moving forward, starting from 2023, discussions regarding the deployment of key roles in examinations will begin earlier, by June, and information will be disseminated to staff in January of the following year to facilitate effective forward planning.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this as 3 sub paragraph in my report: a.	The relief management policy and system were reviewed at SMC in 2022, and the school started using the Edupage Program in 2022. The SMC does relief deployment, post-exam program deployment and exam deployment through the application. The use of technology has improved the work efficiency and staff  benefitted from the convenience of using the application on their computers and mobile devices.
b.	The functions of committees and CCAs, as laid out in the terms of reference, along with the proposed hours and key strengths related to each committee have also been made known to staff. This allows staff to make informed decisions in their choices of committees and CCAs. 
c.	The SLs maintained a open door policy and staff could approach them to raise concerns. At the year-end survey, staff could offer any suggestions for improvement to school policies/processes, including deployment and workload. Since 2022, the feedback received were duly addressed at the year-end staff WPS or Dec Commencement meeting.

a. The relief management policy and system underwent a review by the School Management Committee (SMC) in 2022, leading to the implementation of the Edupage Program. This application facilitated relief deployment, post-exam program deployment, and exam deployment. The utilization of technology significantly improved workflow efficiency, and staff members benefited from the convenience of accessing the application on their computers and mobile devices.

b. The committees and Co-Curricular Activities (CCAs) were clearly defined in their respective terms of reference, which outlined their functions, proposed hours, and key strengths. This transparency allowed staff members to make well-informed decisions when selecting committees and CCAs, aligning their interests and skills with the appropriate areas.

c. Senior Levels (SLs) maintained an open-door policy, encouraging staff members to approach them with any concerns or issues. The end-of-year survey provided an opportunity for staff to offer suggestions for improving school policies and processes, including deployment and workload management. Since 2022, the feedback gathered through this survey has been duly addressed during the year-end staff Work Plan Seminar (WPS) or December Commencement meeting, ensuring that staff concerns are acknowledged, and appropriate actions are taken.

Please help me to paraphrase and summarise this as a paragraph in my report: At 2023 MOE Workplan Seminar, there were several new initiatives announced – refreshed framework for 21CC and student outcomes, launch of EdTech Masterplan 2030, and establishment of MOE Partnerships Engagement Office. The school could explore deployment of staff to look into these areas for better student/staff engagement and to prepare them for life-long learning.

During the MOE Workplan Seminar in 2023, several new initiatives were introduced, which included a revamped framework for 21st Century Competencies (21CC) and student outcomes, the launch of the EdTech Masterplan 2030, and the establishment of the MOE Partnerships Engagement Office. These initiatives present opportunities for the school to consider deploying staff members to focus on these areas. This deployment can contribute to enhancing student and staff engagement, as well as preparing them for lifelong learning.

Hi, please help me to paraphrase and summarise this for my report: The school develops a clear plan/direction towards staff professional development, which aims to develop competent and committed staff, as reflected in ST2. 
a.	In 2020, the school reviewed the strategic plan to be implemented in the next 5 years. Considering the changes in the educational landscape, ST owners proposed the ST and SOs, which were deliberated at the SMC and SPR meetings. Thereafter, staff were invited to brainstorm for strategies that helped to achieve the ST & SOs. Having this whole-school approach in reviewing ST2 provided greater ownership and sense of awareness for staff development. There were also engagement sessions during the yearly workplan seminar to review ST2, where ST owner made adjustments to the plans, considering the feedback obtained from the staff survey.
b.	Each year end, staff complete their LNA survey, which comprised of two sections. 
•	Part A: Teachers do a self-assessment with their ROs to determine their level of practice, based on descriptors in the six areas of practice under SFEd. 
•	Part B: Teachers determine their level of learning needs, based on their own profile.
Using data from the LNA survey, SSD identified key focus areas and planned for the PD of staff at the school-, department- and individual-levels. This applied for the different segments of staff, teachers, KPs and EAS as well. The results from LNA and plans for PD were discussed with SMC before engaging staff at the December Commencement Meeting. The rationale, approach and process for all PD activities were clearly documented in a word document and disseminated to staff at the beginning of each year. This provided clarity and forward planning for all staff. The LNA survey was also shared to the SMC so that ROs could monitor and provide advice to their reportees’ learning and development.

The school has developed a deliberate plan to guide staff professional development, aiming to cultivate competent and dedicated educators, as evident in ST2. 

a. In 2020, the school conducted a review of its strategic plan for the upcoming five years. Recognizing changes in the educational landscape, Subject Team (ST) owners proposed specific strategies and Student Outcomes (SOs) which were thoroughly discussed in School Management Committee (SMC) and School Planning and Review (SPR) meetings. Subsequently, staff members were invited to participate in brainstorming sessions to contribute strategies that aligned with the ST and SOs. This collaborative approach fostered a greater sense of ownership and awareness among the staff regarding their professional development. During the annual workplan seminar, engagement sessions were held to review ST2, during which adjustments were made based on feedback gathered from staff surveys.

b. At the end of each year, staff members complete the Learning Needs Analysis (LNA) survey, consisting of two sections. Part A entails a self-assessment in collaboration with Reporting Officers (ROs) to determine the level of practice based on descriptors in the six areas of practice outlined in the Singapore Framework for Educational Development (SFEd). Part B involves teachers assessing their own learning needs based on their individual profiles. Utilizing the LNA survey data, the School Staff Developer (SSD) identifies key focus areas and plans professional development activities at the school, department, and individual levels. This process applies to various segments of staff, including teachers, Key Personnel (KPs), and Executive and Administrative Staff (EAS). The LNA results and professional development plans are discussed with the SMC and shared with staff during the December Commencement Meeting, providing clarity and enabling forward planning. Documentation detailing the rationale, approaches, and processes of all professional development activities is disseminated to staff at the beginning of each year, ensuring transparency. Additionally, the LNA survey is shared with the SMC, allowing ROs to monitor and provide guidance for their reportees' learning and development.

Hi, please help me to paraphrase and summarise this for my report: The school provides structures and processes for PD in identified key areas of focus.
a.	Time and space were provided for all PD activities. With the implementation of HBL Day, all staff meetings and PD sessions were conducted on HBL Day. This helped streamline staff activities, which used to be held in the afternoons on weekdays. An email would be sent a week before HBL Day so that staff knew their involvement and the respective timing and venue to report.[
b.	A school-wide level training on design and use of rubrics for assessment was conducted in 2022, with heightened importance of performance task to engage our learners and less reliance on pen-and-paper assessment. Two mass workshops were conducted to introduce the rubrics principles, with a 30 minute consultation session in-between workshops for each department/unit. To ensure transference of learning, all IP department planned for at least a WA on performance task for any subject, level or stream each year.
c.	In alignment with the changing educational landscape and MOE initiatives, together with data from LNA, the school focused on three areas of practice under SFEd – ePed, DI and CCE. To ensure that staff has a baseline competency in these areas, coaching sessions were planned for new/beginning teachers, including current teachers who require support in these areas. These teachers would be identified based on the level of practice indicated on SFEd, and also through nominations by HODs. The coaching sessions were helmed by TLs, who were deployed based on their expertise in the respective areas. Following feedback in 2022, the coaching sessions were increased to 6 sessions to better engage the teachers and provide a longer runway for implementation and feedback.
d.	During formal lesson observations, SLs identified teachers who were skillful in delivering e-Ped, DI lessons (with possibility of values-infused). These teachers were then approached to conduct lesson demonstrations in June or Nov/Dec, so that other teachers could benefit from their sharings. Participants were required to provide feedback on the lesson observed to the teacher, and suggest how they would like to apply what they learnt from the lessons. The feedback received will also be given to the teachers so that they could seek improvement and growth for their lessons. [
e.	Teachers were identified by HODs to open their classroom for lesson observations. All teachers were encouraged to sign up for the Open Classroom to observe how authentic teaching and learning could be conducted, specific to e-Ped, DI and values-infused lessons. All teachers were also to observe a peer’s lesson, and be observed for one lesson, with a 3-2-1 reflection to be completed after the peer lesson observation. This provides an open culture of learning where teachers could do so at their own pace and time.
The school has established structures and processes to facilitate professional development (PD
